skill.name: screenshot-feature-extractor
skill.description: Analyze product screenshots, identify visible product features, and turn the findings into structured development task checklists.
skill.summary: Extract product features and development tasks from UI screenshots.
skill.author: notedit

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution, weak-cryptography, and entropy findings are false positives caused by markdown code fences, ordinary wording, and multilingual reference text. The skill has no evidence of shell execution, weak cryptographic use, network access, or prompt injection, but it does direct the agent to write a file using a product-derived filename.

medium_findings.title: User-Derived Output Filename Needs Sanitization
medium_findings.description: The skill asks the agent to write the final task list to docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<product>-features.md. The product name is derived from analysis context, and the instructions do not require path sanitization, so a crafted product value could influence the output path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write target is explicit and includes a product-derived path segment. The risk is moderate because no executable code is present and the intended directory is constrained to docs/plans.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings occur in markdown code fences and prompt examples. They describe agent prompts and output format examples, not executable commands or script code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows only fenced markdown examples and text prompts. No shell command, Ruby execution, or command interpreter invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Static Cryptography Findings Are Text-Match False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain metadata description text, a core principle sentence, a screenshot prompt, and a task description heading. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are plain language and headings. There is no cryptographic API, hash selection, encrypted payload, or credential handling in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Finding Is Multilingual Documentation
low_findings.description: The output format reference contains Chinese examples and markdown formatting. This explains the entropy heuristic without indicating binary, encrypted, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is readable documentation with headings, checklists, and examples. No encoded blob, packed payload, or hidden executable content was found.

content.value_statement: Teams often need to turn UI screenshots into clear product requirements. This skill analyzes visible interface details and creates organized feature checklists for planning.
actual_capabilities: Collects one or more user-provided screenshot paths for analysis.
Separates UI, interaction, and business feature observations into parallel review steps.
Synthesizes separate analyses into a unified development task list.
Formats output as a markdown checklist focused on product behavior.
Deduplicates repeated features across multiple related screenshots.
Reviews the final checklist for completeness and quality.
limitations: It depends on visible screenshot content and user-provided context.
It does not inspect source code or running application behavior.
It may miss hidden workflows, permissions, or backend-only features.
Output filenames should be sanitized before writing to disk.

use_cases.title: Convert Designs into Tasks
use_cases.description: Turn a product mockup or screenshot into a feature checklist for implementation planning.

use_cases.title: Compare Competitor Screens
use_cases.description: Extract visible capabilities from competitor screenshots and identify feature gaps.

use_cases.title: Prepare Development Backlogs
use_cases.description: Create a structured markdown task list from several app screens before engineering estimation.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze One Screenshot
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this screenshot and create a feature checklist from visible UI elements only: [screenshot path].
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner analysis for a single screen

prompt_templates.title: Extract Product Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these screenshots from the same product and generate a deduplicated development task checklist: [screenshot paths].

prompt_templates.title: Run Competitor Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these competitor screenshots, identify visible features, and highlight capabilities our product may need: [screenshot paths].

prompt_templates.title: Create a PRD-Ready Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these UI screenshots, infer modules and user interactions, and write a PRD-ready feature checklist grouped by product area: [screenshot paths].
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for product and engineering teams

output_examples.input: A screenshot of a dashboard with charts, filters, notifications, and account controls.
output: Dashboard overview section with summary metrics and chart panels.
Filter controls for date range, status, and team member.
Notification area with unread state and quick actions.
Account menu with profile, billing, and sign out options.

output_examples.input: Three screenshots showing onboarding, project creation, and a settings page.
output: Onboarding flow with account setup, workspace naming, and progress state.
Project creation flow with validation, templates, and confirmation state.
Settings module with profile fields, notification preferences, and team controls.

best_practices: Provide clear screenshot paths and brief context for each screen.
Group screenshots from the same product when you want deduplicated output.
Review generated filenames before writing the final markdown file.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to infer backend systems that are not visible.
Do not use low-resolution screenshots when detailed UI text matters.
Do not treat the checklist as a final technical architecture.

faq.question: What inputs does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs screenshot file paths and any useful context about the product or workflow.

faq.question: Can it analyze multiple screenshots together?
faq.answer: Yes. It can group related screenshots and deduplicate repeated features across screens.

faq.question: Does it choose a technology stack?
faq.answer: No. The instructions focus on product behavior and avoid implementation details.

faq.question: Can it inspect hidden application behavior?
faq.answer: No. It can only infer features from visible screenshot content and supplied context.

faq.question: Where does it write the output?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the agent to write a markdown task list under docs/plans.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a warning. The skill should sanitize product-derived filenames before writing files.
